# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Kubernetes Deployment manifests with Istio sidecar injection disabled using sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false" annotation. Skip sidecar proxies for certain services that need direct network access without mesh policies. This is for services that require low latency without proxy overhead.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: myapp-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3    # Change this value according to your actual need  
  selector:      # This field is used by Kubernetes for replication of Pods in the same namespace. Also, it defaults to "apps/v1" if no label set (if you don't want labels). Selectors are matched against fields such as spec and status conditions below specified pods - they can select those specific containers within a single container group or an entire statefulset for example
    matchLabels:   # This is the same selector used in your Pod template. It matches any namespace, default label(kubernetes-app=xyz) created by user and managed as per name based deployments that do not start until they have reached 'Ready' status (ie., are ready to serve traffic).
      app: myApp   # Change this value according your actual need  - e.g.: "myMicroservice" or whatever the real service is called in Kubernetes cluster    
---   
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ${POD_NAME}                      # This will be set automatically by kubelet when you create a pod. Change this value if necessary but must match existing `PodName` (i.e., same as the one in your Deployment) otherwise it may interfere with injected sidecar, i.e.: myapp-deployment
  labels:   # This is used by Kubernetes to keep track of Pods and can be set on an application level or specific for each namespace (like default). If not specified then label(k8s in the form) will use based on pod name, if you're managing this at a higher levels like per namespaces.
    app: myApp                           # Change it as needed e.g.: "myMicroservice" or whatever your real service is called - usually matches Deployment  (e.g., same value in case of using managed deployments)    
spec:  
  containers:     
    ${CONTAINER_NAME}:                # This will be set automatically by kubelet when you create a container, change this if necessary but must match existing `ContainerName` according to your Deployment and Pod. You can have different images for each pods in case of using multi-container apps 
      name: ${CONTAINER_NAME}             # This will be set automatically by kubelet when you create a container, change this if necessary but must match existing `ContainerName` according to your Deployment and Pod. You can have different images for each pods in case of using multi-container apps 
      image: nginx                        # Change it as needed e.g.: "myMicroservice" or whatever the real service is called - usually matches Service (e.g., same value)  	      		   	   			         	     	 	        }    					         if you're managing this at a higher levels like per namespaces
  nodeName: ${NODE_NAME}                  # This will be set automatically by kubelet when pod is created, change it according your requirements. For the exact purpose in Kubernetes cluster and not required to match with any existing nodes - leave blank if you are setting this manually for each Pod (i.e., same as node names).
  tolerations:             # This field specifies which nodes the kubelet should target by having those pods tolerated on them, allowing more flexibility in resource usage and load distribution than placing all workloads directly onto a single node  	    		      	     	 	        }     					          If you're managing this at namespace level or higher levels - leave it blank if not needed for your specific case
    key: "node-exporter"                   # This is used by Kubernetes to identify the nodes where pods should run. Must match with NodeLabel as well ie.: `k8s in the form) – e.g., node`, must be a common label shared across multiple services on same cluster and matching all Pods - leave it blank if not needed for your specific case  
    operator: "Exists"                      # This indicates how to match pod with tolerations (i..e.: If you want any of the specified nodes to run thisPod or allow none at all), must be either `In`, and use a value in taints on such matching Pods. - e.g., In
    effect: "Effect"                        # This indicates how long these tolerations should exist (i..e.: If you want any of the specified nodes to run thisPod or allow none at all), must be either `Exists`, and use a value in taints on such matching Pods - e.g., Exists
  affinity:          # This field specifies how pods are scheduled onto hosts (i..e.: If you want any of the specified nodes to run thisPod or allow none at all), must be either `RequiredDuringScheduling`, and use a value in taints on such matching Pods - e.g., RequiredDur...
    requiredDuringScheduling:              # This indicates when pod should not start before scheduling other containers (i..e.: If you want any of the specified nodes to run thisPod or allow none at all), must be either `Preferred`, and use a value in taints on such matching Pods - e.g., Preferred
      nodeSelectorTerms:                   # This contains specifications for which Node(s) should schedule pod (i..e.: If you want any of the specified nodes to run thisPod or allow none at all), must be either `Matching`, and use a value in taints on such matching Pod. - e
  ...                             # Continue with remaining fields as necessary according your requirements   	  		     	 	        }      					          If you're managing these higher levels (like per namespaces) or at namespace level then leave it blank if not needed for this specific case
